rules.name: Product Data Grammar
preconditions: A real product object, lifecycle, evidence type, or content structure is known.
rules.intent: Derive one memorable visual behavior from real product data instead of generic decoration.
constraints: Tie the move to a recurring product meaning.
Use the move consistently and sparingly.
responsiveBehavior: Preserve meaning while simplifying expression on constrained viewports.
accessibility: The signature must remain understandable without motion, color, or fine visual detail alone.
antiPatterns: Unrelated gradient or animation used as a brand substitute.
verification: Name the product datum behind the move and verify the mapping in success and recovery states.

rules.name: Conversion Proof Rhythm
preconditions: Supplied proof or concrete product evidence supports a claim or choice.
rules.intent: Turn the claim-to-evidence-to-action sequence into the composition rhythm.
constraints: Keep evidence adjacent to the claim or choice it supports.
responsiveBehavior: Keep each claim-proof pair intact when sections reorder.
accessibility: Label proof sources and avoid relying on logo recognition.
antiPatterns: Fake testimonials, invented logos, or unsupported metrics.
verification: Trace every proof item to evidence and verify adjacency to its supported claim.

rules.name: Repeated Action Feedback
preconditions: Users repeat a meaningful action on a primary object.
rules.intent: Make immediate state feedback for the repeated action the recognizable product behavior.
constraints: Feedback must reveal the resulting object state.
Keep duration and motion proportional to action frequency.
responsiveBehavior: Keep feedback adjacent to the action and object on every viewport.
accessibility: Expose the result as text and respect reduced-motion preferences.
antiPatterns: Celebratory motion disconnected from actual completion.
verification: Repeat the action with success, pending, offline, and error outcomes and inspect feedback consistency.
